title: Probability - Coins & Dice Quiz
objectives: Understand the fundamentals of Probability - Coins & Dice Quiz.

questions.question: Probability formula is:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Favorable/Total.

questions.question: One coin has:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 2.

questions.question: Two coins total outcomes:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 4.

questions.question: One dice has:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 6.

questions.question: Two dice total outcomes:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 36.

questions.question: Probability always lies between:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 0 to 1.

questions.question: Sure event probability:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 1.

questions.question: Impossible event probability:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 0.

questions.question: Probability of head:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 1/2.

questions.question: Total outcomes of n coins:
questions.explanation: The correct answer is 2^n.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting exactly one head when two coins are tossed?
questions.explanation: Outcomes are HH, HT, TH, TT. Exactly one head occurs in 2 of 4 outcomes = 1/2.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting two tails when two coins are tossed?
questions.explanation: Only TT is favorable out of 4 equally likely outcomes.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling an even number on a fair dice?
questions.explanation: Even faces are 2,4,6 — three favorable outcomes out of six.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a number greater than 4 on a fair dice?
questions.explanation: Numbers greater than 4 are 5 and 6: 2 of 6 outcomes.

questions.question: What is the probability of drawing head then tail in two coin tosses?
questions.explanation: The ordered outcome HT has probability 1/4 among four equally likely results.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a 3 and then a 4 with two dice in order?
questions.explanation: Each die has probability 1/6, so combined probability is 1/6 × 1/6 = 1/36.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a sum of 7 with two fair dice?
questions.explanation: There are 6 favorable outcomes out of 36 possible pairs.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting three heads in three coin tosses?
questions.explanation: Probability = (1/2)^3 = 1/8.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a prime number on a dice?
questions.explanation: Prime numbers on dice are 2,3,5: 3 out of 6 faces.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting at least one head in two coin tosses?
questions.explanation: Only TT has no heads, so 3 of 4 outcomes have at least one head.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a multiple of 3 on a dice?
questions.explanation: Multiples of 3 are 3 and 6: 2 out of 6 outcomes.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling an odd number on a dice?
questions.explanation: Odd numbers are 1,3,5: 3/6 = 1/2.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting exactly two heads in three coin tosses?
questions.explanation: There are 3 favorable outcomes (HHT, HTH, THH) out of 8.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a 1 or a 2 on a dice?
questions.explanation: Two favorable outcomes out of six.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a 5 or 6 on two dice when order matters?
questions.explanation: Each die has 2 favorable faces, so probability = 2/6 = 1/3 per roll.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting all tails in three coin tosses?
questions.explanation: Probability = (1/2)^3 = 1/8.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling two sixes in two dice rolls?
questions.explanation: Probability = 1/6 × 1/6 = 1/36.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a sum of 11 with two dice?
questions.explanation: There are 2 favorable outcomes out of 36.

questions.question: What is the probability of getting at least one six in two dice rolls?
questions.explanation: Probability of no six = 25/36, so at least one six = 11/36.

questions.question: What is the probability of rolling a number less than 5 on a dice?
questions.explanation: Numbers less than 5 are 1,2,3,4: 4/6 = 2/3.
